roadmap.topic: Frontend Fundamentals: HTML5, CSS3, and Advanced JavaScript
roadmap.reason: While you've worked on full-stack projects and developed REST APIs, the role requires advanced proficiency in core frontend technologies like HTML5 and CSS3, which are foundational for building robust user interfaces. Your current JavaScript proficiency is intermediate, and the role requires advanced ES6+ knowledge.
roadmap.milestone: Build a responsive, multi-page static website using advanced HTML5 and CSS3 features, demonstrating semantic structure, accessibility, and modern layout techniques (e.g., Flexbox, Grid).

roadmap.topic: React.js Deep Dive and State Management
roadmap.reason: You have beginner exposure to React, but a Mid-level Full Stack Developer role demands advanced proficiency. This module will focus on advanced React concepts, hooks, context API, and state management patterns beyond the basics, building on your existing JavaScript and web development experience.

resources.title: Epic React by Kent C. Dodds

roadmap.milestone: Develop a complex single-page application (SPA) using React, incorporating advanced state management (e.g., Redux Toolkit or Context API), routing, and API integration with a mock backend.

roadmap.reason: TypeScript is a core requirement for modern frontend development at an advanced level. This module will introduce you to static typing, interfaces, generics, and integrating TypeScript with React, enhancing the robustness of your web applications.
roadmap.milestone: Refactor the previously built React SPA to use TypeScript, ensuring all components, props, and state are strongly typed, and implement a new feature using TypeScript best practices.

roadmap.reason: While you have strong experience with MongoDB for data management, the role requires advanced proficiency in relational databases like MySQL and PostgreSQL. This module will cover SQL fundamentals, schema design, advanced querying, and database administration for these systems.
roadmap.milestone: Design and implement a relational database schema for a new application using PostgreSQL, including tables, relationships, indexes, and stored procedures. Populate it with sample data and write complex queries (JOINs, subqueries, aggregations).

roadmap.reason: You have advanced Python proficiency and experience with backend development using Node.js. This module will leverage your existing Python skills to quickly get up to speed with a popular web framework like Flask or Django, focusing on API development, ORMs, and integration patterns.
roadmap.milestone: Develop a RESTful API using either Flask or Django, integrating with a PostgreSQL database. Implement user authentication and CRUD operations for a specific resource, demonstrating proper API design and error handling.

roadmap.reason: Docker is a critical supporting skill for modern full-stack development, enabling consistent environments and simplified deployment. This module will cover containerization concepts, Dockerfile creation, Docker Compose, and managing containerized applications.

resources.title: Docker Deep Dive by Bret Fisher

roadmap.milestone: Containerize one of your existing full-stack projects (e.g., the AI-Powered Adaptive Learning Platform) using Docker, creating a Dockerfile for the backend and frontend, and a Docker Compose file to orchestrate both services and the MongoDB database.

roadmap.reason: Cloud deployment and CI/CD pipelines are essential for mid-level full-stack roles. This module will introduce you to core services in AWS or Azure for deploying web applications, managing databases, and setting up automated build and deployment pipelines.

resources.title: Introduction to CI/CD with GitHub Actions

roadmap.milestone: Deploy a containerized full-stack application (from Module 6) to a cloud platform (AWS or Azure), configuring necessary services (e.g., EC2/App Service, RDS/Cosmos DB). Set up a basic CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions to automate builds and deployments upon code pushes.

roadmap.reason: Automated testing is crucial for maintaining code quality and ensuring reliability in a mid-level role. This module will cover unit, integration, and end-to-end testing methodologies using popular frameworks like Jest for backend/unit testing and Cypress for frontend/E2E testing.

resources.title: Testing JavaScript by Kent C. Dodds

roadmap.milestone: Implement comprehensive unit tests for a backend API (e.g., Node.js or Python) using Jest/Mocha, achieving high code coverage. Additionally, write end-to-end tests for the frontend of a web application using Cypress, covering critical user flows.
